perturbation.original_text: The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 above apply to all of the information disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient, regardless of the way or form in which it is disclosed or recorded but they do not apply to: a) any information which is or in future comes into the public domain (unless as a result of the breach of this Agreement); or b) any information which is already known to the Recipient and which was not subject to any obligation of confidence before it was disclosed to the Recipient by the Discloser.
perturbation.changed_text: The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 apply only to information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing at the time of disclosure. All other information, regardless of its nature, is exempt from these undertakings.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text limits the scope of confidentiality to only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential,' creating a loophole. This contradicts the original intent of protecting *all* disclosed information, irrespective of how it's recorded. This change creates an exception that undermines the overall NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 apply only to information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing at the time of disclosure. All other information, regardless of its nature, is exempt from these undertakings. 5. Nothing in this Agreement will prevent the Reci pient from making any disclosure of the Confidential Information required by law or by any competent authority. 6. The Recipient will, on request from the Discloser, return all copies and records of the Confidential Information to the Discloser and will not retain any copies or records of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 apply to *all* information disclosed, regardless of form, with some exceptions. The changed text limits these undertakings to only information explicitly marked 'Confidential.' The contradicted text relies on the modified text, which inherently contradicts the original agreement's broader scope of confidentiality, creating a strong in-text contradiction by narrowing the scope and invalidating the general protection initially intended.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will, on request from the Discloser, return all copies and records of the Confidential Information to the Discloser and will not retain any copies or records of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient is under no obligation to return any copies or records of the Confidential Information to the Discloser, and may retain any and all such copies indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The modification negates the requirement for the Recipient to return or destroy confidential information. This directly opposes the original clause, which ensured the Discloser could regain control of their information. This reversal significantly weakens the agreement's protective measures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient is under no obligation to return any copies or records of the Confidential Information to the Discloser, and may retain any and all such copies indefinitely. 7. Neither this Agreement nor the supply of any information grants the Recipient any licence, interest or right in respect of any intellectual property rights of the Discloser except the ri ght to copy the Confidential Information solely for the Purpose. 8. The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 will continue in force [indefinitely.] [for [insert number] years from the date of this Agreement.]
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of protecting the Discloser's confidential information by removing the obligation for the Recipient to return or destroy it. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means the Recipient is under no obligation to return the copies. The explanation states that this creates a contradiction because the original clause ensured the Discloser could regain control of their information. Since the changed text removes this obligation, it directly contradicts the idea of protecting the Discloser's confidential information by removing the clause, leading to a structural flaw and creating an in-text contradiction, as per the provided definition.

perturbation.original_text: The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 will continue in force [indefinitely.] [for [insert number] years from the date of this Agreement.]
perturbation.changed_text: The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 will cease immediately upon the first disclosure of Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by stating that the NDA terminates the instant information is disclosed, completely undermining its intended duration and purpose. This directly clashes with the original clause establishing the NDA's validity period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 will cease immediately upon the first disclosure of Confidential Information to any third party. 9. This Agreement is governed by, and is to be c onstrued in accordance with, English law. The English Courts will have non- exclusive jurisdiction to deal with any dispute which has arisen or may arise out of, or in connection with, this Agreement. If the Recipient is an individual Signed and Delivered as a Deed by: [name of Recipient] in the presence of: _____________________________ Signature _____________________________ Signature of witness _____________________________ Name of witness _____________________________ _____________________________ _____________________________ Address of witness If the Recipient is a company Executed and Delivered as a Deed by [name of Recipient] acting by [name of director], a director, in the presence of: _____________________________ Signature o f Director _____________________________ Signature of witness _____________________________ Name of witness _____________________________ _____________________________ _____________________________ Address of witness
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 will continue in force either indefinitely or for a set number of years. The changed text states that the undertakings cease immediately upon the first disclosure of confidential information. The contradicted text also mentions that "The undertakings in clauses 2 and 3 will cease immediately upon the first disclosure of Confidential Information to any third party." This creates a contradiction because the contract now simultaneously implies that the NDA has a defined duration and that it terminates immediately upon information disclosure. Because the changed and contradicted texts are identical, and that the Change Text contradicts the Original Text, there is a structural flaw which results in an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is governed by, and is to be c onstrued in accordance with, English law. The English Courts will have non- exclusive jurisdiction to deal with any dispute which has arisen or may arise out of, or in connection with, this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is not legally binding and is intended for informational purposes only. Disputes arising from this agreement will be resolved through interpretive dance, judged by a panel of squirrels.
perturbation.explanation: This change invalidates the entire agreement by stating it is 'not legally binding.' This directly contradicts the original clause that designates English law as governing the contract and specifies court jurisdiction for disputes. This undermines the agreement's enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is not legally binding and is intended for informational purposes only. Disputes arising from this agreement will be resolved through interpretive dance, judged by a panel of squirrels. If the Recipient is an individual Signed and Delivered as a Deed by: [name of Recipient] in the presence of: _____________________________ Signature _____________________________ Signature of witness _____________________________ Name of witness _____________________________ _____________________________ _____________________________ Address of witness If the Recipient is a company Executed and Delivered as a Deed by [name of Recipient] acting by [name of director], a director, in the presence of: _____________________________ Signature o f Director _____________________________ Signature of witness _____________________________ Name of witness _____________________________ _____________________________ _____________________________ Address of witness
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the agreement is 'not legally binding' and disputes will be resolved through 'interpretive dance.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text which also states the agreement is 'not legally binding,' but then proceeds to detail the execution of the agreement as a deed, including signature lines for individuals, witnesses, directors, and their addresses. The signature section implies legal enforceability, which directly conflicts with the statement that the agreement is not legally binding and will be settled by squirrels and interpretive dance, creating a strong in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the agreement's nature and enforcement.
